dictionary_sections.definition: engineering specifications is a source-backed registry entry whose production form is anchored to the reviewed equation \[P_{\text{total}} = \sum_{j=1}^{N} \eta_{\text{total}} P_{j}\]. The entry records the mathematical object, condition, or operator named by the title and prepares it for the locked TZPID four-page layout.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, engineering specifications functions as a local mathematical checkpoint. The canonical equation gives the entry its printable formal spine, while the supporting equations show how the relation is used or constrained inside the surrounding registry arc.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The displayed relation permits the entry to be read as a concrete source-grounded step rather than a registry shell. derivation_layer.source_latex:
\tzpsection{Direct Equation Progression}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item \textbf{Canonical Relation}
\[
\[P_{\text{total}} = \sum_{j=1}^{N} \eta_{\text{total}} P_{j}\]
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for engineering specifications.

\item \textbf{Support Relation}
\[
$\Phi_{\times} = \text{Interference}[\text{DualPhase}]$
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for engineering specifications.

\item \textbf{Closure Relation}
\[
\[\text{Access} = \text{Academic} \lor \text{Non-Commercial} \implies \text{MERL-50}\]
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for engineering specifications.

\item \textbf{Auxiliary Relation}
\[
\[T(t) = T_{\text{env}} \exp(-\alpha t) \implies T_{\text{bath}} \le 4.2 \text{ K}\]
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for engineering specifications.

\item \textbf{Review Relation}
\[
$\text{Unity} := \text{Volume}_{00} \oplus \text{Volume}_{01} \implies \text{Status: COMPLETE}$
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for engineering specifications.

\end{enumerate}

\tzpsection{Proof}
The proof route is review-conditioned: verify that the listed source equations are mutually admissible in the entry's TRAWIN-normalized frame.
